name: How I built my portfolio website
postBody:
As a web designer or developer it is so important to have an eye-catching website to show potential clients your work. In this blog post, I'll share how I built my own portfolio website. I'll also include some tips on how you can create your own portfolio website.

The design of your portfolio is one aspect that can make or break the deal when it comes time to pitch potential clients. It needs to be eye-catching, informative and impressive - all at once.

So, if you're looking for a way to improve your personal portfolio website, keep reading!

‍

‍

Short Story About Me:

When I first decided to start coding, I had no idea what I was doing. I spontaneously signed up for a coding bootcamp before even owning a computer, I struggled with the material they taught me to say the least. Despite all the challenges I went through, I didn't give up. I kept learning and eventually stumbled upon Webflow - Which changed the course of my web development career forever.

Although I struggled with the material I was taught at the coding bootcamp, I was able to get a basic understanding of HTML & CSS. This made it much easier for me to pick up Webflow, which is the visual website builder I used to build my portfolio.

1. What tools and software I used to create my website

Webflow 🔥

If you have seen any of my YouTube videos, you know I am a freak about Webflow. Webflow is the software I used to create this website, and it genuinely is the best portfolio websites I made over my quest to become a junior developer. 

I have made what feels like dozens of portfolios over my time, and this one with Webflow has been my favorite, and has delivered the best results with finding new clients for my web design freelancing career.

‍

‍

How Does Webflow Work?

Webflow is a click-and-drag web design tool that made it incredibly easy for me to build responsive websites 100x faster than I was with the knowledge I learned at a coding bootcamp. Because I had an understanding of HTML & CSS, the learning curve really easy with Webflow.

one thing about me is, I have always had a small eye for design, but it was only when I started building websites that I realized how much I truly enjoyed it. 

The first dozen or so websites I was built were awful, absolutely horrendous, and I knew I needed to find a way to make them look better. I learned pretty quickly I didn’t want to become a jr developer and do traditional programming, I wanted to peruse this journey of making websites that stand out and try my hand at freelancing (Watch my video).

I started purchasing dozens of css courses online and watching counties hours of Design Course on YouTube. One lucky day I came across a course on Udemy for freelance web designers. I had never heard of Webflow before, but the course was fantastic, and it completely changed my approach to web design. 

I was finally able to build the beautiful websites that I had always wanted to build, in a fraction of the time it was taking me before using JavaScript frameworks. 

I'm glad that I stuck with the coding bootcamp even though it was tough. (My story) Without that foundation, It would have been a bit more challenging to use Webflow. (But not impossible. Checkout their online university to learn Webflow 101)

Is JavaScript Required?

When I first started learning web development, I was determined to become a “developer”. I spent countless hours practicing my code and learning the skills. However, over time my focus shifted to other areas of web development, and my Javascript skills began to slip. I no longer had any need to use Javascript, as Webflow's built-in CMS made it easy to do everything I needed without writing a single line of code. While there are both pros and cons to using Webflow, it was the best option for me at the time, given my lack of experience with other web development frameworks. Now that my skills have progressed, I may revisit Javascript in the future; but for now, Webflow meets all of my needs.

‍2. How I designed the layout and chose colors

Starting thoughts

When it came to designing my portfolio website, I wanted to create a clean and simple layout  that was eye-catching, informative and impressive - all at once. I also needed something to showcase my work in the best light possible. 

It was easy to get started with a live project, and I was able to experiment with different layouts until I got it just right.

Planning Process

While a wireframe is normally my starting point for a website, I will admit that my portfolio website was put together more quickly and without as much planning. Even so, it has been successful in generating new business. I think potential clients are impressed with the way it looks, especially the dark theme. I think my portfolio has been effective in demonstrating my skills and helping me to land new clients. In the future, I may spend more time on the planning phase, but for now, this quick and dirty approach is working well.

Layout Inspiration

Coming up with a website layout can be tough. You want something that's going to look good and be easy to use, but you also don't want it boring and to appeal to potential clients.

For me, the hardest part has always been the home page. I always want to make sure that the hero section looks perfect, because that's usually the first thing people see when they come to my site. As I was browsing on Dribbble I found another website design that had a logo similar to mine, and I loved the way they showcased it on the hero section. I decided to use that as inspiration for my own site, and I'm really happy with the results. The logo is lightly watermarked in the top right corner of the hero page section, and I think it looks great to have some detail, while also keeping it minimalistic.

Color Inspiration

When it came to choosing colors, I turned to a few places for inspiration. The Webflow Template Library & Dribbble.com are my two main sources for color inspiration inspiration. I wanted to use a dark color scheme that would be easy on the eyes, yet stand out.  After looking through dozens of examples, I settled on a simple black and red theme. I think these colors help to create a professional atmosphere, which is ideal for scoring new business. Overall, I'm very pleased with how my portfolio website turned out, and I'm more than pleased with the way it has helped me to attract new clients and commissions for my freelancing business.

3. Creating each page of my website

After coming up with the perfect color template for my portfolio website, and having a general idea of the layout - I simply started building. I mentioned before this is not my ideal way of doing it, but I have built so many portfolios that I just wanted to take a shot at randomly building with the skills I learned to see what happens.

Homepage

One of the most important elements of any website is the homepage. The homepage is the first thing that visitors see when they arrive at a site, so it's important to make a good impression. I wanted my homepage to be simple and professional, so I chose a basic layout style and included only the most essential information. I think that this approach makes it easy for visitors to find what they're looking for and navigate my site. In addition, I believe that it helps to create a more positive overall experience for users.

Other Pages

I originally started my portfolio with just a single page, but I quickly realized that wouldn't be enough to really showcase my work. I've been working as a web designer for a few  years now and I have gained a lot of experience, so I wanted my portfolio to reflect how my skills have grown from leaving the bootcamp. I added pages for each of the different types of website work I do, as well as a page highlighting some of my favorite projects. I also included a blog and my YouTube videos so potential clients can get to know me better. 

My portfolio is always a work in progress, but I'm very happy with how it's turned out so far.

‍

Recommended pages for your website

When designing your site, there are a few key pages that you should include. 

-Homepage

The homepage is the first impression that visitors will have of your site, so it's important to make sure it's well-designed and informative. 

-About

The about page is your chance to tell visitors who you are, what you do, and why they should hire you. 

-Services

The service page should clearly outline the services that you offer.

-Contact

and the contact page should provide visitors with a way to get in touch with you. 

-Portfolio

Finally, the portfolio page is where you can showcase your previous work and showcase your talents. 

By including these key pages on your site, you can make sure that you're providing potential clients with all the information they need to make a decision about hiring you.

4. Tips for anyone who wants to create their own portfolio website

Web design is one of the most important aspects of your work as a web developer. (Ok maybe not all, but for most)  As such, it's essential that you take the time to create a website that is both visually appealing and easy to navigate.

Here are a few tips to help you get started:

1. Keep it simple. 

Your portfolio website should be easy to navigate and free of clutter. Stick to a simple design that highlights your work in the best possible way.

2. Choose your platform wisely. 

There are a number of different platforms you can use to build your portfolio website, so take some time to research your options before settling on one. 

You know I like Webflow.

But other options consist of

  • Wix
  • Squarespace
  • Wordpress
  • HTML & CSS
  • GoHighLevel
  • So much more

3. Make it mobile-friendly. 

In today’s world, it’s important to make sure your website can be accessed from any device. Make sure your portfolio website is responsive and looks great on all screen sizes.

4. Use high-quality images. 

When it comes to portfolios, visuals are key. Be sure to use professional-looking images that showcase your work in the best light possible.

5. Write compelling copy for your website. 

In addition to strong visuals, your portfolio website needs well-written copy. 

I struggled with writing copy for a long time, but if you made it this far in the blog post I feel like you should know my secret for writing copy - AI does it. Now I offer it as an up-charge for my clients. 

Conclusion: 

Thank you for taking the time to read my short story about how I designed my portfolio as a freelance web developer/designer. If you’re looking to build a portfolio website and want an easy way to do it, I recommend using Webflow. I am being completely genuine when I say it's been life changing for me. 

You can use their library of templates or start from scratch, and it has some great features like built-in hosting and domain name registration.

If you have any questions for me, please feel free to reach out to me through my website. 

Thanks for reading! 🤙🏽
postSummary: The design of your portfolio is one aspect that can make or break the deal when it comes time to pitch potential clients. It needs to be eye-catching, informative and impressive - all at once. So, if you're looking for a way to improve your personal portfolio website, keep reading!

name: How I Make Passive Income Selling Websites ($150 a day)
postBody:
How I Make Passive Income Selling Websites ($150 a day)

In this article, I'm going to explain to you how I make $150 a day (passively) selling websites. 

‍Quick Into:

What’s going on everyone. My name is Joe webb. And In this article I want to share with you how I turned one of my website side projects into making on average $150 a day on autopilot.

For those of you that don’t know me. My name is Joe. I have been designing and developing websites for around 4 years now - and if you have seen any of my youtube videos you know I was off to a rocky start. But I knew if I stuck with it. The skills would pay off in the end - and today I want to share with you one of my side projects. 

I have built so many website side projects to make money over the years - but this is the first time I actually wanted to write about it. (And eventually talk about it on YouTube.)

To be completely honest. I have debated on writing this for a while now. I run the small risk of stunting the growth of this project by saying what I'm about to say. I am also aware $150 a day isn’t all that much, but I think it’s an achievable number for anybody.

I genuinely enjoy helping people. And it’s just natural for me to share things that have been beneficial to me in my life or mybusiness.

*This wont work for everyone

I think what I got to work for me will only work for a handful of people out there. It takes a special kind of person, and it needs to align with your current goals and skill set.

I like to be honest in all my articles and videos. No BS'ing you, or beating around the bush. I just like to tell you how it is and what my experience has been like.

So the side project that makes me $150 a day - is selling website & funnel templates in a platform called GoHighLevel.

I Sell Website & Funnel Templates to Marketers

I have briefly mentioned in my YouTube videos what GoHighLevel is, but I will explain it a little more here.

GoHighLevel Websites

Watch a quick demo: GoHighLevel is an all-in-one marketing platform.

How I got Started

I got introduced to it about 2.5 years ago when I started working with a marketing agency with a few of my friends. They needed help with website and funnel designs - and I was also interested in learning digital marketing.

Before I was building Websites exclusively within GoHighLevel, I used  another platform called Webflow for all my projects. That platform gave me the skill I needed to start building websites I was proud of and I could start selling for a decent profit. 

What I do Now

I have since parted ways with my marketing friends to pursue web development, but remember I couldn't shake my mind from the opportunities I saw within GoHighLevel. This has since become my go-to platform for almost all my new clients. 

Over time I saw how much faster I could build in GHL, with what appeared to be the same level of work in the eyes of the client.

New opportunities for me and my clients

Using GoHighLevel has given me the opportunity to provide so many additional services to my website clients that were previously very complicated to piece together. (At a reasonable price for the client)

Better quality websites for my clients

When I first got started freelancing websites to business owners, I quickly learned my client wasn’t always that happy with my websites when they find out people don’t just magically find it on the internet without actively promoting it. A lot of clients think computers are magic. And they expect certain things from most websites these days…. Building a website with all the features a client expects from scratch takes a considerable amount of time.

The services I offer with GoHighLevel Websites

Now I can give my clients a full CRM with their new website that is built directly within the same platform. For those of you that are reading/watching this that subscribed from my coding bootcamp days, this is basically a full stack website I can offer. I love the ability to offer so many services while spending a fraction of the cost and time to build it. 

If you subscribed to me from my coding bootcamp days this might make more sense to you. GoHighLevel has the websites and funnels (the front end) that the user sees - but also has the database and automations. (which is basically the backend stuff). Although the user doesn't see it they expect it to happen. 

Features includes

  • Free Hosting
  • Custom Automations
  • Forms
  • Surveys
  • Calendars
  • Email Marketing
  • Test message marketing
  • Pipelines
  • Dashboards
  • Analytics
  • Automated Review Management
  • Blogs (Coming before the end of the month supposedly)
  • And so much more

GoHighLevel has everything I ever needed to help a client make money with their website, build right into it.

What’s the point of a company's website If it’s not making money?

Example Website I build for clients

I always tell a client that a website is essentially useless if it's not making you money. So a good example is a website I made for a chiropractor. One thing is for sure, he needs people to schedule appointments so he can make money.

Ability to Schedule appointments

The scheduling of appointments with built in calendars is a must. I would include a form and calendar for the user  to schedule a time with the chiropractor, (or business owner). 

Confirmation texts or emails

After the scheduled time is fired off a series of text & or emails saying “thanks for scheduling a time” - and based off the time they schedule they will receive appointment reminders messages.

Automated follow up & appointment rescheduling

After the scheduled time passes - the client will receive an email asking if the client showed. If responds with “no” - a new series of messages are sent to the client encouraging the user to reschedule. 

Automatic revenue tracking

If the client did show up to the appointment - the business owner enters how much they were paid, and they can keep track in a dashboard of how much money is being generated.. This is visual proof that my website is generating their business money.

Sell Your Automations As A Software

The final cherry on top of GoHighLevel is the ability to white label all of these automations - and package them up into your own little bundles and sell it as your own software service.

This is where I see the opportunity for web developers and designers to capitalize on some extra side money.

The secret opportunity for web designers/developers

That’s the brief overview of the platform, and I make $150 a day automatically by selling pre-designed website & funnel templates - this is on top of what I charge to build out a full custom account for a client.

I specifically build websites within GHL and sell them to everyone trying to sell their own white labeled version. Or to various business owners that finds genuine value in what GoHighLevel has to offer and enjoy doing the work themselves.

Why people buy my GoHighLevel Templates

I have learned that 7/10 people using GoHighLevel don’t know how to make a website look good. Most of the users are business owners, or freshly started agencies that don’t have a good website developer.

My customers are marketers - not website designers / developers

Luckily for me I have spent the last few years trying to master the art I’d good web design.

I have multiple GoHighLevel templates that people can come in and buy for somewhere between $50-$100 and use for their website or funnel projects.

I average about 2 sales a day and It’s just been a little side project to gauge how many people actually want better designed websites & funnels within their GoHighLevel accounts.

Why am I even telling you about GoHighLevel?

I wanted to share this with you only because I think GoHighLevel is awesome! It’s changed the course of my business forever. But I’m also into marketing and just web sites and anything web related - this platform has allowed me to do so much I never thought I would be able to do for clients and their businesses. And I really mean that. Plus - My clients absolutely love how easy it is to use for them. I have completely changed the course of dozens of my clients businesses, and it was because I was able to provide so much more then just a new website.

Not just a website - A digital asset for a company

I will admit GoHighLevel is a little complicated to work with. I think a lot of other YouTuber and bloggers out there fail to mention that. But if you know how to be self motivated and teach yourself new skills (or copy youtubers) you'll pick it right up.

From my personal experience, after you get the first few accounts set up. It’s really not hard to start cloning and just slightly re-working everything to fit the next client.

Why so many people are using GoHighLevel lately

Another bonus to GHL is, you become an affiliate by default. What I have personally noticed is, because everyone becomes an affiliate by default - AND - because anyone can white label the platform as your own.. There are soooooo many people out there promoting GoHighLevel of their own white labeled version of it, to business owners. 

Thousands of affiliates with 0 website experience

I will admit I thought this was a bad thing at first. That was until I realized how few of these people starting to use GoHighLevel knew how to make websites and funnels that even look half decent. 

An opportunity for somebody that's ready

I personally believe the amount of people out there promoting GoHighLevel is where the opportunity is for website designers and developers.

If it’s something you want to check out, then of course I’m going to drop the affiliate link they gave me and I’d appreciate it if you used it. 

I genuinely love this platform. And I know for those of you that aren't like other people and choose to work for yourself, you can make it successful if you tackle it with the right approach.

I know there are other platforms out there that do the same thing. And I assume a lot of what I’m saying here could apply there to those platforms.

Join me on building websites for digital marketers

But because I’m a true fan of GoHighLevel - I’ll drop a link down below with a little info to their website explaining it. They also give me a link I can share that gives you 2 weeks for free.  

Get two weeks for free of GoHighLevel

So definitely use that if you are actually interested. 

Start a website business in GoHighLevel with my templates

And just as my own personal bonus. If you sign up through my affiliate link - I will GIVE you access to all of my templates I make, for free. (This will be set before the end of July. Email me for direct links to templates)

Personal or business Use

You can use them for personal or for business. You can use these to jump start your own website business like I have. What ever you want to do with them, you can.

Conclusion:

I hope you found some value in this. If you have any questions about getting started with GoHighLevel, or have questions about starting your own web design business with my templates- please send me an email through my website

I don’t upload very often (to youtube or my blog) - because there are a lot of YouTuber that are just talkers - and I’m actively trying to do - before I turn around and start telling others what to do - I only like to upload when I genuinely have something worth sharing.. I’m just a regular guy. Just like you. Trying to find ways to make money from my website skills.  And this has been my own personal jackpot - Not necessarily the $150 a day from templates. (That’s gas money at this point.)

Looking to work yourself as a web developer or digital marketer?

But if you are that one person, just like me, who can't stand to have a boss, and needs to find a way to work for themselves. This is where I would start if I had to do it all again.

Ask me anything

If you want me to make any sort of follow up video(or blog) - Please leave a comment on my video or send me an email and I’d love to start making videos and blogs based around some of your questions you might have for me.

‍

Thanks again for reading

I’ll catch you in the next one ✌🏽 

‍
postSummary: I want to share with you how I turned one of my website side projects into making on average of $150 a day on autopilot .
